items.title: MCP Server Architecture Patterns for LLM-Integrated Applications
items.abstract: The Model Context Protocol (MCP), introduced by Anthropic in November 2024, defines a standardized interface for connecting large language models (LLMs) to external tools, data sources, and services. Within months of release, hundreds of community-built MCP servers appeared on GitHub, but no softwar...

items.title: Always-OnAgents:A Survey of Persistent Memory, State, and Governance in LLMAgents
items.authors: Tianyu Ding, Aditya Nannapaneni, Bingfan Liu et al.
items.abstract: Always-on agents are systems whose future behavior depends on durable state accumulated across earlier interactions. We treat them as persistent-state systems: the operative system includes retrievable memories, but also task ledgers, permissions, credentials, commitments, provenance and audit recor...

items.title: DialogPII: A multilingual dataset of synthetic dialog transcripts to detect personal information
items.authors: Roland Roller, Vera Czehmann, Derya Erman et al.
items.abstract: Conversational data collected in domains such as healthcare or social sciences is a valuable resource for research and automated analysis. However, responsible data sharing requires the detection and removal of personally identifiable and sensitive information to protect individual privacy. To suppo...

items.title: When Is a Draft Accepted? A Theory of Acceptance in Speculative Decoding
items.abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates language model inference by using a fast drafter to propose candidate tokens that are then verified by a larger target model. Existing theory largely studies the stochastic, distribution-preserving setting, where the goal is to exactly sample from the target distribu...

items.title: Optimizing Image Preparation and Compression for Face Recognition within 1024 Bytes
items.authors: Paul Andreas, Torsten Schlett, Christoph Busch
items.abstract: ICAO-compliant machine readable travel documents enable automated biometric face verification. The biometric reference is stored on an RFID chip included in form of a JPEG or JPEG 2000 compressed facial image. In contrast, temporary travel documents lack of machine readability, which excludes the ow...

items.title: BrainJanus: A Unified Model for Understanding and Generation across Brain, Vision, and Language
items.authors: Haitao Wu, Qirui Zhang, Zhouheng Yao et al.
items.abstract: Modeling the bidirectional correspondence between external sensory stimuli and internal neural activity has emerged as a critical frontier in neuroscience. However, existing approaches predominantly treat brain encoding and decoding as isolated tasks, relying heavily on unimodal alignment and extern...

items.title: Hybrid Active-Online Learning Framework for Label-Efficient Concept Drift Adaptation in Optical Network Failure Detection
items.authors: Yousuf Moiz Ali, Jaroslaw E. Prilepsky, João Pedro et al.
items.abstract: We propose a hybrid active-online learning framework for label-efficient concept drift adaptation in optical network failure detection. Using margin-based selective labeling, our method achieves nearceiling accuracy and AUC scores while querying only 3.4% of streaming samples, with negligible latenc...

items.title: Chronos: A Physics-Informed Full-History Framework for Non-Markovian Long-Horizon Manipulation
items.authors: Yulin Zhou, Yimeng Wang, Nengyu Wang et al.
items.abstract: General-purpose robot policies should be modeled as dynamical systems, yet many VLA and generative imitation policies still rely on present observations or short windows. This Markovian shortcut fails in memory-dependent manipulation: identical observations can demand different actions after differe...

items.title: CSAR: Containerized System Architecture for Robotics
items.authors: Ambrosio-Cestero, Gregorio, Galindo Andrades et al.
items.abstract: Robotic applications increasingly rely on distributed computational infrastructures that combine embedded devices, edge servers, and cloud resources. This evolution, together with the collaborative nature of robotics projects, has made the development, integration, deployment, and long-term operatio...
